name: Neuroendocrine and Systemic Signal Reset Coverage Repair Assessment

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair from the prior thin coverage assessment through the current repair pass.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair adjudicated the named S1PC/eNAMPT and klotho gene-therapy gaps, staged a bounded public bundle for PMID 42102814, NCT07216781, and NCT07285629, and checked brief eNAMPT/review triangulation without broadening the evidence map. Coverage is not strong because TRIIM-X and klotho gene-therapy programs still lack posted results, S1PC functional evidence is animal-level with human biomarker context only, and the track remains mechanistically heterogeneous with unresolved safety and attribution boundaries.

evidence_categories.rationale: TRIIM anchors the public human_biomarker_signal stage, TRIIM-X remains a randomized no-results registry, and the repair adds two no-results klotho gene-therapy trial-watch registries. This is adequate for the current low-confidence biomarker-stage claim but not for functional benefit.
evidence_categories.gap_note: No controlled human functional or durable clinical outcome is available.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current public claim is intervention-stage and does not rely on observational or natural-experiment evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Observational endocrine or circulating-factor associations may provide context but should not upgrade the intervention stage.

evidence_categories.rationale: The evidence map includes central hypothalamic lifespan and aging-phenotype studies, hypothalamic stem-cell exosome aging studies, systemic oxytocin aged-muscle regeneration, aged-rhesus klotho cognition, and staged S1PC/eNAMPT aged-mouse functional support.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The animal evidence remains heterogeneous and does not define one deployable modality.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline covers endocrine combination therapy, hypothalamic inflammatory-neuroendocrine signaling, hypothalamic secretory exosomes, systemic oxytocin, and klotho protein signaling. Repair adds S1PC/LKB1/SIRT1/adipose eNAMPT to hypothalamic signaling.

The outlook and staged repair explicitly capture the main limiting points: TRIIM is small and uncontrolled, TRIIM-X and klotho registries have no posted results, S1PC is animal functional plus human biomarker context, and none of the animal or NHP signals proves durable human function.
evidence_categories.gap_note: There is no definitive negative randomized human result yet.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repaired map now explicitly flags safety and durability constraints for recombinant growth hormone combinations, S1PC/eNAMPT, klotho protein, and klotho plasmid or combination gene-therapy delivery. Coverage is adequate for conservative claims but not strong because results are missing.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Actual klotho gene-therapy safety data remain pending.

evidence_categories.rationale: TRIIM-X is captured and rechecked, and the repair stages NCT07216781 and NCT07285629 as no-results klotho gene-therapy trial-watch records.

Repair checked recent review and eNAMPT/NAD World context enough to confirm that PMID 42102814 is part of a coherent adipose-hypothalamic eNAMPT branch. No review-level public source is needed for the current bounded claim.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A deeper citation-network review could improve strong coverage, but is not required before ordinary surveillance resumes.

evidence_categories.rationale: Major anchors for TRIIM, hypothalamic NF-kappaB/GnRH, hypothalamic stem-cell exosomes, klotho NHP cognition, oxytocin aged muscle, and the S1PC/eNAMPT branch are represented or explicitly triaged for future context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: PMID 31204283, PMID 38194970, PMID 39870672, and PMID 41285826 are recorded as context for possible future deeper eNAMPT coverage rather than staged in this bounded bundle.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged outlook and intervention records explicitly separate TRIIM, S1PC/eNAMPT, klotho protein, klotho plasmid gene therapy, klotho/follistatin combination gene therapy, oxytocin, and hypothalamic interventions, while noting overlap with DNA methylation, NAD/redox, metabolite, circulating-factor, niche-support, and gene-therapy tracks.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair checked PubMed source metadata and abstracts for PMID 42102814 plus eNAMPT triangulation, direct ClinicalTrials.gov API records for NCT07216781 and NCT07285629, and broader web search for klotho/eNAMPT/TRIIM context. Non-primary web hits were treated as leads only.
source_selection_notes: PMID 42102814 was staged as the bounded public S1PC/eNAMPT repair source.
NCT07216781 and NCT07285629 were staged as no-results trial-watch records rather than efficacy support.
PMID 31204283, PMID 38194970, PMID 39870672, and PMID 41285826 were reviewed as eNAMPT branch context but not staged in this bounded repair.
Commercial Minicircle and secondary web pages were not used as evidence; ClinicalTrials.gov supplied the verifiable registry facts.

known_gaps.description: TRIIM-X remains recruiting with no posted results despite a stale estimated completion date, so the main human follow-up signal is still unresolved.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Recheck NCT04375657 and PubMed during ordinary surveillance.

known_gaps.description: The klotho plasmid and klotho/follistatin plasmid gene-therapy registries are recruiting or early no-results records, so they cannot support benefit, durability, or safety claims yet.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Watch NCT07216781 and NCT07285629 for posted results or peer-reviewed reports.

known_gaps.description: No reviewed intervention shows randomized human functional, intrinsic-capacity, disease, durability, mortality, or LEV-relevant benefit from a defined neuroendocrine or systemic-signal reset modality.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Do not upgrade beyond human_biomarker_signal until controlled human functional or durable clinical outcomes appear.

known_gaps.description: Growth hormone combinations, DHEA/metformin, hypothalamic manipulation, GnRH, S1PC/eNAMPT, oxytocin, klotho protein, and gene-therapy delivery each have different preventive-use safety and durability constraints.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require modality-specific safety, dosing, durability, and active-component evidence before increasing confidence.

known_gaps.description: S1PC/eNAMPT, klotho, TRIIM, oxytocin, and hypothalamic interventions overlap NAD/redox, metabolite restoration, circulating factors, niche support, DNA methylation, nutrient sensing, and gene-therapy framing.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep future public changes explicit about which evidence supports neuroendocrine/systemic signal reset itself versus adjacent mechanisms.

next_coverage_action: Review and publish the staged coverage-repair bundle if evidence reviews pass; after publication, return neuroendocrine and systemic signal reset to ordinary surveillance.
